mcqs.question: In a cloud environment, the principle of least privilege (PoLP) ensures that users are granted:

options.text: Full administrative access to all cloud resources for efficiency
Minimum permissions necessary to perform their specific job functions
Temporary elevated access that never expires for emergency use
Same access level as their manager to simplify permission management

mcqs.explanation: The principle of least privilege limits user access to only what is strictly required for their role, reducing the attack surface and potential for unauthorized access.

mcqs.question: A company stores sensitive customer data in AWS S3. Which encryption approach ensures data is protected both at rest and in transit when accessed by authorized applications?

options.text: Only using SSL/TLS for data in transit
Only encrypting data at rest using server-side encryption
Enabling server-side encryption at rest (SSE-S3) and enforcing HTTPS for all access
Relying on the cloud provider's default unencrypted storage

mcqs.explanation: Comprehensive data protection requires encrypting data at rest (using SSE-S3) and in transit (using HTTPS/TLS) to prevent interception and unauthorized access.

mcqs.question: Which compliance framework requires cloud service providers to implement specific technical safeguards for protecting electronic protected health information (ePHI) in multi-tenant cloud environments?

options.text: PCI-DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard)
HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act)
ISO 27001 (International Organization for Standardization)
SOC 2 (Service Organization Control 2)

mcqs.explanation: HIPAA mandates specific administrative, physical, and technical safeguards for ePHI, including requirements for cloud providers handling healthcare data.

mcqs.question: A security architect needs to implement network isolation for a multi-tier application running in AWS. Which configuration provides the MOST secure network segmentation?

options.text: A single VPC with all resources in the public subnet
Separate VPCs for each tier using private subnets and security groups with restrictive inbound rules
One large subnet with firewall rules at the application layer only
Using public IP addresses for all database servers for easier management

mcqs.explanation: Isolating tiers into separate private subnets within a VPC with security groups creates defense-in-depth, limiting lateral movement if one tier is compromised.

mcqs.question: Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) tools primarily address which security challenge in cloud environments?

options.text: Detecting malware in running containers
Identifying and remediating misconfigurations across multi-cloud infrastructure
Blocking unauthorized user login attempts
Encrypting data at the field level in databases

mcqs.explanation: CSPM continuously monitors cloud environments for security misconfigurations, compliance violations, and infrastructure risks, providing automated remediation.

mcqs.question: An organization discovers that several departments are using unsanctioned SaaS applications (shadow IT). Which cloud security approach is MOST effective in addressing this risk?

options.text: Implementing a Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) to discover and control shadow IT usage
Blocking all internet access to prevent unauthorized application use
Deploying additional physical firewalls at network entry points
Conducting annual security audits without real-time monitoring

mcqs.explanation: CASB solutions sit between users and cloud services to provide visibility into SaaS usage, enforce security policies, and protect against shadow IT threats.

mcqs.question: A microservices application uses Kubernetes for container orchestration. Which Cloud Workload Protection Platform (CWPP) capability is MOST critical for securing containerized workloads at runtime?

options.text: Static vulnerability scanning of base images before deployment
Runtime behavioral analysis and container sandboxing to detect malicious activity
Only monitoring network traffic between containers
Manual security patches applied quarterly

mcqs.explanation: Runtime protection with behavioral analysis detects anomalies and threats in running containers, while sandboxing isolates workloads to prevent container escape attacks.

mcqs.question: In a Zero Trust security model for cloud infrastructure, which statement correctly represents the core philosophy?

options.text: Trust but verify - internal network traffic is considered trusted by default
Never trust, always verify - every access request is authenticated and authorized regardless of network location
Trust verified users completely after initial authentication
Only enforce security controls at the network perimeter

mcqs.explanation: Zero Trust operates on the principle of 'never trust, always verify,' requiring continuous validation of identity, device health, and context for every resource access request.
